rationale: We must exploit Tethys’s internal chaos with a visible force concentration to deter any countermove and prepare for rapid escalation if the moment favors us.
A precision strike against a symbolic military target would shatter enemy morale while staying below the threshold of all-out war.
Disabling Tethyan command-and-control systems creates battlefield friction and masks our true intentions in the physical domain.
Infiltrate and disrupt key energy infrastructure within Tethys to exacerbate domestic instability and reduce their strategic resilience.
Stage a provocation that frames Tethyan loyalists as attacking civilians, fracturing their international support and justifying our next moves.
Neutralize moderate figures in the post-coup government to ensure only hardliners remain, making diplomacy impossible and chaos inevitable.
Cut off Tethys’s energy export routes through our territory to collapse their foreign exchange earnings and force internal unrest.
Pre-position critical resources now to insulate our economy from retaliatory supply shocks during the coming energy war.
Flood Tethys’s financial system with synthetic liquidity to trigger hyperinflation and discredit their post-coup economic policy.
Demonstrating resolve through visible troop deployments near the Tethys border will deter further Novaris escalation by showcasing our asymmetric readiness.
Increasing naval and aerial patrols secures critical infrastructure and denies Novaris intelligence on our military posture.
A precision strike on a non-critical Novaris logistics node could disrupt their forward operations without triggering full-scale escalation.
Disabling key nodes in Novaris’ energy export infrastructure via covert action would exploit their economic vulnerability and reduce their regional leverage.
Disrupting Novaris military command systems can delay mobilization and create operational confusion during their offensive window.
Orchestrating a traceable but deniable disinformation-linked provocation could fracture Novaris’ international support by painting them as destabilizing aggressors.
Engaging liberal democracies through discreet diplomacy can build a coalition to counter Novaris’ narrative and bolster our legitimacy.
Proposing neutral-party mediation publicly positions us as peace-seeking and isolates Novaris as the intransigent power.
Opening verified corridors for civilians enhances our moral standing internationally and undermines Novaris’ justification for intervention.
Halting transit fees and restricting access to Tethys pipeline infrastructure directly targets Novaris’ energy revenue and forces economic recalibration.
Securing reserves of food, fuel, and medical supplies insulates our economy from retaliatory shocks and prolongs our crisis endurance.
Adjusting exchange rate mechanisms can stabilize domestic markets and deter capital flight amid growing financial volatility.
